cells.source:
# Day 1: Python Basics Practice

Topics:
- Variables
- Data Types
- Operators
- Type Conversion

## 1. Variables
Variables are used to store data in memory.

x = "Ankit"
y = "boy"
z = 20

print("My name is", x)
print("I am a", y)
print("I am", z, "years old")

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.text:
My name is Ankit
I am a boy
I am 20 years old

cells.source:
##2. Data Types
Common data types in Python:
- int → whole numbers
- float → decimal numbers
- str → text
- bool → True or False

name = "Ankit"   # str
age = 20         # int
price = 99.5     # float
is_student = True  # bool

print(type(name))
print(type(age))
print(type(price))
print(type(is_student))

## 3. Arithmetic Operators
Basic math operations in Python.

a = 2
b = 5

print("Addition:", a + b)
print("Subtraction:", a - b)
print("Multiplication:", a * b)
print("Division:", a / b)
print("Floor Division:", a // b)
print("Power:", a ** b)
print("Modulus:", a % b)

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.text:
Addition: 7
Subtraction: -3
Multiplication: 10
Division: 0.4
Floor Division: 0
Power: 32
Modulus: 2

cells.source:
## 4. Comparison Operators
These are used to compare two values.

a = 20
b = 6

print("a < b:", a < b)
print("a > b:", a > b)
print("a == b:", a == b)
print("a != b:", a != b)
print("a <= b:", a <= b)
print("a >= b:", a >= b)

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.text:
a < b: False
a > b: True
a == b: False
a != b: True
a <= b: False
a >= b: True

cells.source:
bottle_price = 20
extra_charge = 5

total = bottle_price + extra_charge

print("Final Bill is:", total)

## 6. Common Mistake and Fix
You cannot add a string and an integer directly.

apples = 5
label = "Count: "

# Wrong way:
#print(label + apples)

# Correct way:
print(label + str(apples))

## Day 1 Reflection

Today I learned:
- Variables store data
- Data types have different meanings
- Operators do math and compare values
- String and integer cannot be added directly without conversion

I found difficult:
- Type conversion at first

Tomorrow I will:
- Practice more examples
- Revise today’s topics again
